mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly and efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring fast query performance on large datasets. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse

```

> This is the official repository for ClickHouse.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for ClickHouse to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/master/deploy/kubernetes/crds/clickhouse.altinity.com_clickhouses.yaml

```

> This CRD is essential for managing ClickHouse clusters.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> This installs ClickHouse with the specified version.

steps.title: Create ClickHouse Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource for ClickHouse to define the cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  shardsCount: 1
  replicasCount: 1
EOF

```

> This defines a simple ClickHouse cluster with one shard and one replica.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  shardsCount: 1
  replicasCount: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command removes the ClickHouse release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to be drained.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to be uncordoned.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the ClickHouse server is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the ClickHouse pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available for query'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no available ClickHouse replicas to handle the query.

**Fix:**
Verify the status of the ClickHouse replicas with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens due to misconfiguration or resource limits being exceeded.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n clickhouse' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the ClickHouse pod cannot be scheduled due to lack of available resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough resources or adjust the resource requests/limits in the ClickHouse configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage engines.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to review the new SQL functions and adjust queries accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse' and its status should be 'Running'.

  replicasCount: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with sufficient resources.
